title: Die chliine Wörtli
desc: Discourse particles that glue it together

sn.s: Es git Wörtli, wo fascht nüt bedüted und doch alls.
sn.e: There are little words that mean almost nothing and yet everything.

sn.s: "Gäll" hänkt mer ane, wenn mer Zuestimmig wott.
sn.e: You add 'gäll' when you want agreement.

sn.s: "Schöns Wätter, gäll?" heisst: "oder findsch nöd au?"
sn.e: "Nice weather, gäll?" means: "don't you think too?"

sn.s: "Halt" seit mer, wenn öppis eifach so isch.
sn.e: You say 'halt' when something just is the way it is.

sn.s: "Es isch halt so" heisst: "mer cha nüt mache."
sn.e: "It's just like that" means: "nothing can be done."

sn.s: "Scho" cha "zwar" heisse: "es isch scho guet, aber..."
Eis säuber heissed sie wenig. Zäme mached sie de Ton.
sn.e: On their own they mean little. Together they make the tone.

sn.s: Wer sie richtig setzt, tönt sofort wie vo da.
sn.e: Whoever places them right sounds instantly local.

sn.s: Mer cha sie nöd us em Buech lärne.
sn.e: You can't learn them from a book.

sn.s: Mer mues sie ghöre und eifach probiere.
sn.e: You have to hear them and just try them out.

sn.s: Jetz rütsched sie mir ganz vo sälber use.
sn.e: Now they slip out of me all by themselves.

sn.s: "Eba, gäll," säg ich, und denke gar nöd meh dra.
sn.e: "Eba, gäll," I say, and don't even think about it anymore.
